action.reasoning: Observation: train.py and predict.py still use buggy collate_fn passing list of tensors (variable length) to Wav2Vec2FeatureExtractor causing the inhomogeneous shape ValueError on np.array internally. No training/eval submitted yet after 3 iters; last command only inspected predict.py. Analysis: Root cause confirmed in collate (waveforms from torchaudio are tensors of diff lengths); fix is explicit .numpy() conversion to list of ndarray. Batch prints violate stdout rules. Decision: Edit train.py (fix collate, remove per-batch prints, bump train batch to 32) + predict.py (fix collate, bump batch to 64), run 2-epoch training for baseline, then immediately submit_eval on best.pt to obtain first test EER and measure per-iter time.
